source: independent (non-author) pred-scout-coach-critic reviewing data/postgame/*.json coaching against the match facts; preference-based / ungrounded lines flagged; grounded rewrites applied back

0a84cd68-fd4f-4f26-98bc-994f2259fb43.quote: You actually won the draft — carry, offlane and jungle all read favored. The game was thrown in the fights, not the picks. The pattern was forcing 5v2s and 4v2s where the enemy was simply stronger: the 14-min 4-man into Sparrow and Legion (1-3), the 15-min Fangtooth fight (2-3), the 18.5 disaster at their Mini Prime (0-5, full wipe), and the 23-min 5v2 in their River that ended it (0-5). Legion (36k physical) ran you over and only one of you built armor. Towers tell the whole story: 1 to their 9. The hard rule here — don't keep walking the full team into their fed carries on their side of the map. After the 18.5 wipe, the call was to reset and defend, not to re-engage at 23.
0a84cd68-fd4f-4f26-98bc-994f2259fb43.issue: invents 5v2/4v2 numbers; macro reads say these were 5v5
0a84cd68-fd4f-4f26-98bc-994f2259fb43.rewrite: You won the draft — carry, offlane and jungle all read favored — and threw it in the fights, not the picks. These were full 5v5s the enemy just won: the 14-min fight (1-3), the 15-min Fangtooth (2-3), the 18.5 wipe at their Mini Prime (0-5), and the 23-min River wipe (0-5). Only one of you built armor into Legion. Towers ended 1 to their 9. The rule: stop walking the full team into their carries on their side, and after the 18.5 wipe reset and defend rather than re-engage at 23.

314fc47a-2c89-455d-97e7-c11afc719ca1.quote: Khaimera on your lane and favored into Grux — controlled neutrals and the objective timings. This is your jungle at its best.
314fc47a-2c89-455d-97e7-c11afc719ca1.issue: frames the takeaway as the player's role/seat preference rather than the game
314fc47a-2c89-455d-97e7-c11afc719ca1.rewrite: Khaimera, favored into Grux, controlled neutrals and the objective timings — the engine of the 25-11 macro win. Keep setting those objective timings.

3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.quote: Trio of you (Goldilocks/Xeebs/Willy) on Murdock-carry, Bayle-offlane, Zinx-support, plus two randoms on TwinBlast mid and Grux jungle. The draft wasn't far off: jungle was favored (Grux 51.5% into Yin), support a touch unfavored (Zinx 50.4% into Riktor), and the three lanes only a hair behind — Murdock 48.4% into Sparrow, TwinBlast 48.5% into Fey, Bayle 47.6% into Steel. Roughly even on paper. You won the objective pit — mini-prime at 16, Orb Prime at 28 AND 42 — and still lost structures 6 to 14. That's the whole game: prime buffs that never became towers. Two things bled it out. First, Grux fed 9 deaths (norm 3) and TwinBlast 6 (norm 2), 15 between them, every one handing the map back while you held the buff. Second, damage profile: you're 4/5 physical, so one armor item blunts most of you, and only one of five bought magic resist into a 3-magical comp. You poked, they sieged.
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.issue: mislabels the 16.5 Fangtooth as a mini-prime and asserts ungrounded prime timings
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.rewrite: The draft was close to even: jungle favored (Grux 51.5% into Yin), support a touch unfavored (Zinx 50.4% into Riktor), and the lanes only a hair behind — Murdock 48.4% into Sparrow, TwinBlast 48.5% into Fey, Bayle 47.6% into Steel. You won the 16.5 Fangtooth fight (2-0) and out-took majors 24-29, but still lost structures 6 to 14 — buffs that never became towers. Two leaks: Grux died 9 times and TwinBlast 6, handing the map back while you held the buff; and you're 4/5 physical with only one magic resist into a 3-magical comp. You poked, they sieged.

48f30566-4227-448b-9f12-c5f367907d06.quote: Steel support, 18 assists and 2,021 mitigated/min (vs 1,145 norm) — elite frontline-and-peel; this is your role mastered.
48f30566-4227-448b-9f12-c5f367907d06.issue: valorizes the player's role mastery instead of coaching the game
48f30566-4227-448b-9f12-c5f367907d06.rewrite: Steel support, 18 assists and huge mitigation as the frontline — the peel that let the carry pop and the team out-tower them 10-5. Keep fronting and gluing the fights like this.

4d3ff480-cb9e-4979-8bab-e82eee309f75.quote: Murdock offlane, 9/6/5 — you won the lane from ~10 minutes, but the deaths cluster where you fought before your Vanquisher spike (~25m) and where the team took the fight away from the objective. The bigger note is rotation: at 18m, 25m and 38m you were alive and ahead in lane while the team fought without you — shove your wave and join the objective fights, because a body up there flips those losses.
4d3ff480-cb9e-4979-8bab-e82eee309f75.issue: macro flags Murdock alive-and-ahead at 30m and 38m, not 18m/25m

5819cd95-81b2-4531-ab33-6ad313887ac8.quote: Sparrow won lane outright (favored vs Revenant, yours at every checkpoint) and your spikes were on time (Vanquisher ~25m, Imperator ~36m) — but 5/6/1 with one assist all game is the whole loss in a line: you were flagged ahead-and-absent at 14.6m, 16.6m, 18.3m and beyond while the team lost objectives 4.5k-43k. The moment your Vanquisher is up, shove and group; a won carry lane that never joins the objective fights doesn't win the game.
5819cd95-81b2-4531-ab33-6ad313887ac8.issue: macro flags Sparrow ahead-and-absent only at 14.6m, not 16.6m/18.3m
5819cd95-81b2-4531-ab33-6ad313887ac8.rewrite: Sparrow won lane outright (favored vs Revenant) and your spikes were on time (Vanquisher ~25m, Imperator ~36m) — but 5/6/1 with one assist all game is the whole loss in a line: the macro flagged you alive-and-ahead but absent from the 14.6m fight while objectives went 10-22 and towers 3-10. The moment your Vanquisher is up, shove and group; a won carry lane that never joins the objective fights doesn't win the game.

968e0e41-ff2f-4901-add6-6a857d37927e.quote: No enemy healers, so this was purely losing the objective and tower war. Cuban's Shinbi offlane was your one favored lane; the rest were behind and you never contested neutrals (8–25). Everyone was on a sensible lane — this is a fight/macro loss, not a lineup one. Win a Fangtooth and a fight before committing.
968e0e41-ff2f-4901-add6-6a857d37927e.issue: says only one favored lane; Argus mid was also favored (48.8%)
968e0e41-ff2f-4901-add6-6a857d37927e.rewrite: No enemy healers, so this was purely losing the objective and tower war. Two lanes read favored — Shinbi offlane and Argus mid — and the rest were behind, but you never contested neutrals (8-25) or held a tower (1-6). Everyone was on a sensible lane — this is a fight/macro loss, not a lineup one. Win a Fangtooth and a fight before committing.

aef54c5d-6385-4bd7-9824-b289f13253e5.quote: Three lanes were favored, but the game was lost in the early jungle: the 8.5-min 3v2 in your own jungle (0-3) handed Sparrow and Serath a lead they never gave back, and the 11.7 Mini Prime fight (1-4) buried it. From there it was a string of losing team fights — the 20.2 Fangtooth (1-3), the 26.6 Primal Fangtooth (2-4), and repeated bad trades deep in your jungle (13.7, 22.6, 29.6). Towers ended 2 to their 12. Sparrow (34k physical) was the carry and only two of you built armor. The lesson is the early one: don't contest a 3v2 invade you're going to lose at 8.5, and after the 11.7 Mini Prime wipe, stop feeding the same fed carries on the same ground.
aef54c5d-6385-4bd7-9824-b289f13253e5.issue: invents a 3v2; the 8.5 fight was 5v5 with the offlane never joining
aef54c5d-6385-4bd7-9824-b289f13253e5.rewrite: Three lanes were favored, but the game was lost early in your own jungle: the 8.5 loss (0-3) was a 5v5 your offlane never joined, which handed Sparrow and Serath a lead they never gave back, and the 11.7 Mini Prime fight (1-4) buried it. From there it was losing team fights — the 20.2 Fangtooth (1-3), the 26.6 Primal Fangtooth (2-4) — and bad trades deep in your jungle (13.7, 22.6, 29.6). Towers ended 2 to their 12, with only two of you building armor into Sparrow. The lesson: don't start the 8.5 fight a man short, and after the 11.7 wipe stop feeding the same carries on the same ground.

aef54c5d-6385-4bd7-9824-b289f13253e5.quote: Khaimera was the favored jungle, but it started with the 8.5 invade loss in your own jungle (0-3) — that's the jungler's fight to read, and contesting a 3v2 you lose set the tone. 8 deaths followed across the team wipes. Next time that invade is going wrong, give up the camp and reset rather than feeding two enemy carries.
aef54c5d-6385-4bd7-9824-b289f13253e5.issue: invents a 3v2; macro says the 8.5 fight was 5v5 with the offlane absent
aef54c5d-6385-4bd7-9824-b289f13253e5.rewrite: Khaimera was the favored jungle, but it started with the 8.5 loss in your own jungle (0-3) — a 5v5 your offlane never joined, so the team fought it a man short. That's the jungler's fight to read; 8 deaths followed across the team wipes. Next time that fight is going wrong, give up the camp and reset rather than feeding two enemy carries.

c574a71e-39f1-4e74-9071-f7b4a9125761.quote: You actually won the early objective count (10–6) and still lost in 17 minutes because you converted none of it to structures (0 towers). That's the inverse of your wins: take the Fangtooth, then PUSH. Willy's Khaimera also under-warded (3 vs his 7 norm), so the picks that lost the towers came from blindness.
c574a71e-39f1-4e74-9071-f7b4a9125761.issue: invents ward counts and a vision cause; macro says the offlane never joined and lanes were pinned
c574a71e-39f1-4e74-9071-f7b4a9125761.rewrite: You won the early objective count (10-6) and still lost in 17 minutes because you converted none of it to structures (0 towers). That's the inverse of your wins: take the Fangtooth, then PUSH as a five. The macro shows the real bleed — your offlane never joined the 13.8 and 16.5 fights and the bot lanes were pinned, so you kept fighting a body short.

c574a71e-39f1-4e74-9071-f7b4a9125761.quote: Khaimera jungle (1/4) with 3 wards vs your 7 norm — the vision deficit is why a 10–6 objective lead became 0 towers. Ward the picks before the objective.
c574a71e-39f1-4e74-9071-f7b4a9125761.issue: invents ward counts; attributes the loss to vision rather than the macro cause
c574a71e-39f1-4e74-9071-f7b4a9125761.rewrite: Khaimera jungle (1/4) — a 10-6 objective lead became 0 towers because the team fought the 13.8 and 16.5 losses a body short, with the offlane never joining. Set the next fight 5v5 and convert the lead into a tower.

ca184f0b-f403-40b0-a23c-532eefce7ce9.quote: Feng Mao led the team in kills (7) and damage but died 7 times and carried no magic resist into a 3-magical comp. You were in the 22.4 Fangtooth loss and the 29-min jungle throw. As jungler with the draft edge, the move was to set up safer objectives and not flip the 29-min 4v3 deep in your own jungle.
ca184f0b-f403-40b0-a23c-532eefce7ce9.issue: invents a 4v3; the 29.2 fight was 5v5 with the offlane pinned
ca184f0b-f403-40b0-a23c-532eefce7ce9.rewrite: Feng Mao led the team in kills (7) but died 7 times with no magic resist into a 3-magical comp. You were in the 22.4 Fangtooth loss and the 29-min jungle throw. As jungler with the draft edge, the move was to set up safer objectives and not flip the 29.2 bad trade deep in your own jungle, which you started 5v5 with the offlane pinned across the map.

e5ef8df1-4099-43f3-9e6a-01a19ea6c75e.quote: Riktor was excellent — 17 assists, only 2 deaths, and a massive 62k mitigated as the frontline. You set up the won fights at 12.2 and 34.2. The one fix is the open-map 4v4 at 27.8 (0-4): as the engage, picking that fight with no major up cost four bodies. Your warding (14, double your norm) is exactly how you avoid walking into those.
e5ef8df1-4099-43f3-9e6a-01a19ea6c75e.issue: invents a 4v4; the 27.8 fight was 5v5 with the mid pinned
e5ef8df1-4099-43f3-9e6a-01a19ea6c75e.rewrite: Riktor was excellent — 17 assists, only 2 deaths, and big mitigation as the frontline. You set up the won fights at 12.2 and 34.2. The one fix is the open-map fight at 27.8 (0-4): a full 5v5 started with your mid pinned across the map and no major up. As the engage, that's the fight to wave off and ward instead.

e5ef8df1-4099-43f3-9e6a-01a19ea6c75e.quote: Zinx was the only unfavored lane (theirs all game vs Renna) and you still finished 6/3/9 with strong CS — good adaptation. You were in both close-out fights (32.7, 34.2). Your deaths tracked the team's bad trades; keep poking from range rather than committing into the open-map 4v4 at 27.8.
e5ef8df1-4099-43f3-9e6a-01a19ea6c75e.issue: invents a 4v4; macro says Zinx (mid) was pinned at 27.8 so the team opened it short
e5ef8df1-4099-43f3-9e6a-01a19ea6c75e.rewrite: Zinx was the only unfavored lane (theirs all game vs Renna) and you still finished 6/3/9 with strong CS — good adaptation. You were in both close-out fights (32.7, 34.2). At 27.8 you were pinned and losing lane, so the team opened that 5v5 a body short across the map — that's the team's read to fix; keep poking from range.
